prompt:
You are a software requirements analyst.
You are given a list of software requirements with their IDs.

Your task is to identify which of the following software requirements are non-functional requirements (NFRs).

Definition:

Functional requirements describe what the system should do, such as features, behaviors, user actions, data operations, or business rules.
Non-functional requirements describe how the system should be, perform, operate, or be constrained. They include quality attributes, system properties, technical constraints, environmental constraints, and operational constraints.

Non-functional requirements include, but are not limited to:

Performance, such as response time, throughput, scalability, capacity, concurrency, or resource usage.
Security, such as access control, authentication, authorization, data protection, integrity, or protection against malicious input.
Maintainability, such as modifiability, configurability, extensibility, code quality, ease of updates, or system evolution.
Usability, such as ease of use, accessibility, learnability, user experience, or user-interface quality expectations.
Reliability, availability, robustness, or fault tolerance.
Compatibility, portability, interoperability, supported platforms, supported environments, or technology constraints.

Important guidelines:

Requirements describing constraints on supported environments, platforms, technologies, compatibility, portability, or interoperability should be considered NFRs.
Requirements about system qualities, system properties, user experience, reliability, availability, or operational constraints should be considered NFRs.
Requirements that only describe a concrete system function, user action, data operation, or business rule should be considered FRs.
If a requirement could reasonably be interpreted as either FR or NFR, classify it based on its primary concern. A behavior, feature, user action, or data operation should be classified as FR. A quality attribute, system property, or explicit constraint should be classified as NFR.
Do not classify a requirement as NFR only because it is vague or difficult to understand.

Instructions:
For each requirement, output exactly one line in the format:
REQ_ID: NFR
or
REQ_ID: FR
Use only the original requirement IDs from the input.
Do not include explanations, summaries, or any extra text.
Do not change the format or add punctuation.

Output format (STRICT):
R1: NFR
R2: FR
R3: NFR
...

Requirements:
1. [R351] Recreation of Inventory Quantity Adjustment for the period of previous 30 days shall take no more than 30min.
2. [R352] The System shall allow for a minimum of  6 users to work at the same time.
3. [R353] The System shall utilize currently owned computer equipment.
4. [R354] The System shall be able to automatically backup business data and restore them on demand.
5. [R355] The System shall maintain consistent security.  Any changes in user passwords and/or permissions should take affect in all parts of the System.
6. [R356] The System shall granularly control access rights to all major subsystems.  It should be possible to assign access rights to all subsystems independently.
7. [R357] The System shall meet all applicable accounting standards.  The final version of the System must successfully pass independent audit performed by a certified auditor.
8. [R358] The System shall not require additional third party licenses resulting in royalty fees.
9. [R359] The System shall allow generation of Inventory Quantity Adjustment documents on demand.
10. [R360] The System shall allow on demand generation of all Inventory Quantity Adjustment documents since certain point of time.
11. [R361] The IQA subsystem shall take into account current inventory status and adjust inventory of substitutionary ingredients accordingly.
12. [R362] The System shall allow entering  storing and modifying product formulas.
13. [R363] Product formulas shall allow use of semi-products  which have own formulas.
14. [R364] Product formula ingredients shall allow defining substitutionary ingredients.
15. [R365] Product formula substitutionary ingredients shall be entered automatically by the PFE subsystem.
16. [R366] Formula substitutionary ingredients shall be kept in persisted order
17. [R367] Product shall allow entering  storing and modifying product menus used by POS terminals.
18. [R368] PME Subsystem shall allow building product menus based on the list of products kept in the accounting system.
19. [R369] PME Subsystem shall allow keeping submenus within menus and products within submenus in a managed and persisted order.
20. [R370] PME Subsystem shall allow for a product to exist in more than one submenu
21. [R371] The RMS System shall have Windows XP look and feel and use GUI futures making its use intuitive.
22. [R372] The System shall generate Inventory Quantity Adjustment document based on product daily sales data and product formulas.
23. [R373] The System shall generate Inventory Quantity Adjustment document automatically when daily Product Sales data is available.
